skill.name: azure-kubernetes
skill.description: Plan, create, and configure production-ready Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters. Covers Day-0 checklist, SKU selection (Automatic vs Standard), networking options (private API server, Azure CNI Overlay, egress configuration), security, and operations (autoscaling, upgrade strategy, cost analysis).
skill.summary: Plan, create, and configure production-ready Azure Kubernetes Service clusters with best practices.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Security audit completed. All static findings were evaluated as false positives. The skill is official Microsoft Azure documentation providing AKS cluster planning guidance. No malicious patterns, credential handling, or prompt injection detected.

medium_findings.title: External Command References in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Skill contains documentation references to Azure CLI commands. These are legitimate documentation patterns, not executable code. The analyzer incorrectly flagged markdown table entries and CLI command references as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Azure CLI commands in markdown documentation tables are standard documentation patterns, not executable backtick code. The analyzer misidentified documentation formatting as code execution patterns.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Reference
low_findings.description: Skill contains a hardcoded URL to Microsoft Learn documentation for Azure CNI Overlay. This is legitimate reference documentation from an official Microsoft source.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL points to official Microsoft Learn documentation (learn.microsoft.com) for Azure CNI Overlay feature. This is appropriate reference material for an AKS skill.

content.value_statement: Provision and configure Azure Kubernetes Service clusters with best-practice settings for networking, security, and operations. Simplifies complex AKS decisions with expert guidance.
actual_capabilities: Plan AKS cluster architecture with SKU selection (Automatic vs Standard)
Configure AKS networking including Azure CNI Overlay, pod IP models, and egress
Implement AKS security with Microsoft Entra ID, Workload Identity, and Azure Policy
Set up AKS observability with Managed Prometheus, Container Insights, and Grafana
Design AKS upgrade and patching strategy with maintenance windows
Optimize AKS costs with Spot node pools, auto-scaling, and Reserved Instances
limitations: Cannot execute kubectl commands directly - provides guidance and CLI commands only
Requires Azure subscription with appropriate permissions for cluster operations
Does not manage workloads running inside the cluster after deployment

use_cases.title: DevOps engineers provisioning new clusters
use_cases.description: Get step-by-step guidance for creating production-ready AKS clusters with proper networking, security, and cost optimization from the start.

use_cases.title: Cloud architects designing AKS infrastructure
use_cases.description: Understand Day-0 vs Day-1 decisions, choose appropriate SKU tiers, and plan networking and identity architecture for enterprise workloads.

use_cases.title: Platform teams standardizing Kubernetes deployments
use_cases.description: Establish consistent AKS deployment patterns across teams with documented best practices for security, monitoring, and upgrade strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic AKS cluster
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new AKS cluster for [production/dev] workloads in [region]. I need [3] availability zones and plan to run [web applications/background workers].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a simple AKS cluster with minimal customization

prompt_templates.title: Design AKS networking architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the networking for an AKS cluster with private API server, Azure CNI Overlay, and controlled egress through Azure Firewall.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need guidance on AKS networking options and configuration

prompt_templates.title: Configure AKS security hardening
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure security hardening for our AKS cluster including Microsoft Entra ID integration, Workload Identity, Azure Key Vault secrets, and Azure Policy enforcement.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to implement security best practices for an existing cluster

prompt_templates.title: Plan AKS upgrade and maintenance strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an upgrade and maintenance strategy for our AKS cluster running production workloads. Include maintenance windows, auto-upgrade settings, and zero-downtime procedures.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to plan cluster maintenance without downtime

output_examples.input: Create a production AKS cluster in eastus with high availability
output_examples.output:
For a production AKS cluster in eastus with high availability, I recommend:

**SKU Selection**: AKS Automatic (recommended for production with Node Auto-Provisioning)

**Configuration**:
- Availability zones: 1, 2, 3 for zone-redundant control plane
- Network plugin: Azure CNI Overlay (pod IPs from private overlay range)
- Dataplane: Azure CNI powered by Cilium (eBPF-based performance)
- System node pool: 2x Standard_D4ds_v5 (4 vCPUs, 16GB RAM)

**Identity**: Microsoft Entra ID with Workload Identity (no static credentials)

**Security**: Azure Policy + Deployment Safeguards enabled

**Observability**: Managed Prometheus + Container Insights + Grafana

**Cost estimate**: ~$280/month for control plane + node compute

output_examples.input: What networking options should I choose for AKS?
output_examples.output:
**AKS Networking Options Guide**:

| Option | Best For | Pod IPs | VNet Routable |
|--------|----------|---------|---------------|
| Azure CNI Overlay | Most workloads (recommended) | Private overlay range | No |
| Azure CNI (VNet) | Pods must be directly addressable | From VNet subnet | Yes |

**Recommendations**:
- Default to Azure CNI Overlay for cost efficiency and scale
- Use Azure CNI VNet only when pods require direct VNet access
- Enable LocalDNS on node pools for reliable DNS resolution
- Configure egress through Static Egress Gateway for predictable IPs

output_examples.input: How do I secure my AKS cluster?
output_examples.output:
**AKS Security Best Practices**:

1. **Identity**: Use Microsoft Entra ID everywhere + Workload Identity for pods
2. **Secrets**: Store in Azure Key Vault via Secrets Store CSI Driver
3. **Images**: Only allow signed images via Azure Policy + Ratify
4. **Network**: Enable network policies, restrict ingress/egress
5. **Encryption**: Enable etcd encryption at rest, TLS for node communication
6. **Monitoring**: Enable Microsoft Defender for Containers + Diagnostic Settings
7. **Governance**: Apply Azure Policy for compliance guardrails

best_practices: Start with AKS Automatic SKU unless you need specific customizations not supported by Node Auto-Provisioning
Use Microsoft Entra ID with Workload Identity instead of static service principals or kubeconfig secrets
Plan Day-0 decisions carefully (networking, API server access) as these are hard to change after cluster creation
anti_patterns: Do not use kubeconfig files with static credentials - use Microsoft Entra ID authentication with short-lived tokens
Avoid B-series burstable VMs for production workloads - they cause reliability issues under variable load
Do not skip availability zones - always deploy across 3 zones for production to ensure SLA compliance

faq.question: What is the difference between AKS Automatic and Standard SKU?
faq.answer: AKS Automatic provides a curated experience with Node Auto-Provisioning, pre-configured best practices, and managed node lifecycle. Standard gives you full control over node pools and configuration but requires more manual management.

faq.question: Can this skill create an AKS cluster for me?
faq.answer: This skill provides expert guidance, generates Azure CLI commands, and helps you understand the options. Actual cluster creation requires running the commands in your Azure environment.

faq.question: What networking model should I choose for AKS?
faq.answer: Azure CNI Overlay is recommended for most workloads as it simplifies IP management and scales well. Use Azure CNI VNet only when pods must be directly routable from the VNet or on-premises.

faq.question: How do I handle AKS secrets securely?
faq.answer: Use Azure Key Vault with the Secrets Store CSI Driver to mount secrets as volumes in pods. This avoids storing secrets in etcd or environment variables.

faq.question: What VM sizes should I use for AKS nodes?
faq.answer: Use VMs with at least 4 vCPUs for production workloads. Avoid B-series burstable VMs. Prefer latest generation SKUs (Dv5, Dsv5) for better performance and cost efficiency.

faq.question: How do I reduce AKS costs for dev/test environments?
faq.answer: Use Spot node pools for interruptible workloads (up to 90% savings), stop/start dev clusters when not in use, and consider Reserved Instances for steady-state production workloads.
